title: I’ve joined Anthropic
summary:
The provided content does not contain the article titled "I’ve joined Anthropic." Instead, it displays an error message from x.com (formerly Twitter).

The message, presented in both Chinese and English, states that JavaScript is disabled in the user's browser, preventing the website from functioning properly. It advises the user to enable JavaScript or switch to a supported browser to continue using x.com.

Below the error message, the page includes standard website footer links such as Help Center, Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, Cookie Policy, Imprint, and Ads Information. The page concludes with a copyright notice for © 2026 X Corp.

Therefore, the main point of the provided text is a technical error notification from x.com, indicating that the intended article content is inaccessible due to disabled JavaScript.
chinese_title: 我加入了安思乐
chinese_summary:
所提供的内容不包含题为“我已加入 Anthropic”的文章，而是显示了来自 x.com（前身为 Twitter）的错误消息。

该消息以中文和英文两种语言呈现，说明用户的浏览器禁用了 JavaScript，导致网站无法正常运行。它建议用户启用 JavaScript 或切换到受支持的浏览器以继续使用 x.com。

在错误消息下方，页面包含标准网站页脚链接，例如帮助中心、服务条款、隐私政策、Cookie 政策、版本说明和广告信息。页面最后是 © 2026 X Corp. 的版权声明。

因此，所提供文本的要点是来自 x.com 的技术错误通知，表明由于禁用了 JavaScript，预期文章内容无法访问。

title: Gemini 3.5 Flash
summary:
Google DeepMind has launched Gemini 3.5 Flash, the initial model in its new 3.5 series, designed to combine "frontier intelligence with action" for complex, agentic workflows. Released on May 19, 2026, Flash excels in agentic tasks and coding, demonstrating strong performance on long-horizon challenges with real-world applications.

It's immediately available globally through the Gemini app, AI Mode in Google Search, and for developers via Google Antigravity, Gemini API, and enterprise platforms. A more advanced model, 3.5 Pro, is slated for release next month.

Gemini 3.5 Flash offers intelligence rivaling large flagship models but is four times faster, achieving exceptional speed without compromising quality. It outperforms Gemini 3.1 Pro on coding and agentic benchmarks like Terminal-Bench 2.1 (76.2%), GDPval-AA (1656 Elo), and MCP Atlas (83.6%), and leads in multimodal understanding (84.2% on CharXiv Reasoning).

This balance makes it ideal for agentic tasks, significantly reducing time and cost for activities like application development, codebase maintenance, and financial document preparation. Integrated with the Antigravity harness, it can deploy collaborative subagents for multi-step workflows and generate richer, interactive web UIs.

Industry partners including Shopify, Macquarie Bank, Salesforce, Ramp, Xero, and Databricks are already using 3.5 Flash to automate complex workflows and gain insights. It's also the default model for the Gemini app and AI Mode in Search, powering new personal AI agents like Gemini Spark, which helps users navigate and act in their digital lives.

Developed under Google's Frontier Safety Framework, 3.5 Flash incorporates strengthened cyber and CBRN safeguards through advanced safety training and interpretability tools.
chinese_title: 双子座 3.5 闪

title: Tennessee man jailed 37 days for Trump meme wins settlement after lawsuit
summary:
Retired Tennessee law enforcement officer Larry Bushart has won an $835,000 settlement after being jailed for 37 days over a Facebook meme. Represented by the Foundation for Individual Rights and Expression (FIRE), Bushart filed a federal civil rights lawsuit against Sheriff Nick Weems, Investigator Jason Morrow, and Perry County, Tennessee.

Bushart's post, shared after the September 2025 assassination of Charlie Kirk, was a pre-existing meme accurately quoting Donald Trump's "We have to get over it" statement about a school shooting. The meme specifically referenced a 2024 school shooting at Perry High School in Iowa. Despite this, Sheriff Weems obtained an arrest warrant, claiming the meme could be a threat to Perry County High School in Tennessee, over 500 miles away. Weems later admitted he knew the meme referred to an incident in a different state but omitted this context from the warrant application.

Larry spent over a month in jail on a $2 million bond, losing his post-retirement job and missing personal milestones. His case gained national attention, leading to his release. FIRE senior attorney Adam Steinbaugh stated that no one should be jailed for a harmless meme simply because authorities disagree with its message. The settlement aims to underscore the importance of protecting First Amendment rights and holding law enforcement accountable for their violations. The article also highlights other free speech cases FIRE has taken on following Kirk's assassination.
chinese_title: Tennessee man jailed 37 days for Trump meme wins settlement after lawsuit
